# Drawer

A touch-friendly panel that slides from an edge of the viewport.

## Installation

Add the Drawer component from the configured StackTrace registry.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/drawer
```

## Usage

Use Drawer for a compact, touch-first workflow that should remain tied to the current screen. The default bottom direction provides a visible drag handle; other directions can be selected on the root when the surrounding layout makes another edge more natural.

```vue
<template>
  <Drawer>
    <DrawerTrigger as-child><Button variant="outline">Open drawer</Button></DrawerTrigger>
    <DrawerContent>
      <div class="mx-auto w-full max-w-sm">
        <DrawerHeader>
          <DrawerTitle>Notification settings</DrawerTitle>
          <DrawerDescription>Choose whether product updates should be sent.</DrawerDescription>
        </DrawerHeader>
        <div class="px-4">
          <Field orientation="horizontal">
            <Switch id="drawer-updates" />
            <FieldLabel for="drawer-updates">Product updates</FieldLabel>
          </Field>
        </div>
        <DrawerFooter>
          <DrawerClose as-child><Button>Save preferences</Button></DrawerClose>
          <DrawerClose as-child><Button variant="outline">Cancel</Button></DrawerClose>
        </DrawerFooter>
      </div>
    </DrawerContent>
  </Drawer>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { Button } from '@/Components/Button'
import {
  Drawer,
  DrawerClose,
  DrawerContent,
  DrawerDescription,
  DrawerFooter,
  DrawerHeader,
  DrawerTitle,
  DrawerTrigger,
} from '@/Components/Drawer'
import { Field, FieldLabel } from '@/Components/Field'
import { Switch } from '@/Components/Switch'
</script>
```

## Structure and actions

Include a `DrawerTitle` and use `DrawerDescription` when more context is needed. Place the primary and cancel actions in `DrawerFooter`. Wrap actions that should dismiss the panel with `DrawerClose`; do not rely on the swipe gesture as the only way to leave.

`DrawerTrigger` and `DrawerClose` accept `as-child`. Preserve the focusability and merged attributes described in the [composition fundamentals](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/fundamentals/composition).

## State and direction

Bind `v-model:open` when an external event must control the drawer. The underlying drawer root also forwards interaction options such as `direction` and snapping behavior. Keep drag behavior predictable, and test scrollable content on a touch device so scrolling does not feel like an accidental dismissal.

## Responsive use

A drawer is not automatically responsive; choose it deliberately at the breakpoint where touch-oriented bottom-sheet behavior is preferable. When rendering Dialog on larger screens and Drawer on smaller screens, keep the title, description, fields, and actions equivalent so changing viewport size does not change the task.

> **Use Dialog for compact desktop workflows: [Dialog](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/dialog)**
>
> Dialog is usually the clearer choice when the workflow is centered, keyboard-focused, and does not benefit from dragging or an edge-based mobile presentation.

Hosted documentation: [Drawer](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/drawer)
